title: senecillis dictyonura
parts: Senecillis dictyonura has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Senecillis dictyonura are rich in sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant contain glycosides, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The flowers of Senecillis dictyonura contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and carotenoids, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
side_effects: Senecillis dictyonura can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use can lead to liver damage, as well as kidney problems, and even death in severe cases. The plant's toxic glycosides can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, and may interact with other medications, exacerbating their side effects. Improper use of Senecillis dictyonura can also lead to respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath and coughing.
